---
title: ""
format: 
  html:
    pagetitle: Homepage
    toc: False
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <style>
          button { padding: 10px 20px; background-color: #2c2c2c; color: white; border: none; cursor: pointer; border-radius: 8%;}
          button:hover { background-color: #8a8a8a; }
          </style>
---

Recent grad looking for work in the Atlanta or NoVA area.  

## Projects:  
### [Big-B-Router ![](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/pickpj/Big-B-Router?color=232323&logo=github&labelColor=232323)](https://github.com/pickpj/Big-B-Router/)  
Editing a PBF file to avoid ALPR cameras. The specific file type enables support with other map applications (Osmand). Which means support for niceties such as cross platform support (ios/google-play/f-droid/carplay/android auto), turn-by-turn routing/rerouting, and rich customizability, all whilst avoiding ALPRs.  
  
---  
### [ev-morse ![](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/pickpj/ev-morse?color=232323&logo=github&labelColor=232323)](https://github.com/pickpj/ev-morse)  
Reading linux event files for advanced macro activation based on morse pattern key presses. Works independent of the windowing system (wayland/x11) and is device specific. Pattern based activation enables multiple macros to be activated by the same key. Ev-morse can also handle repeating activations with longer key presses (like a fast forward button).     

## Recent Articles:  
[Bigbrouter](./Mapping/FIock/bigbrouter.qmd)  
Step by step guide to using Big-B-Router  
[Bad Cams - No Road Overlap](./Mapping/FIock/diff.qmd)  
Identifying flawed OpenStreetMap data for the whole of the US.  
[Bridges](./Mapping/FIock/bridges.qmd)  
Identifying bridges/rivers to be ALPR surveillence chokepoints/checkpoints  

## Contact:  
```{=html}
    <button onclick="
        (function() {
            const s1 = 'c2VjbHVzaXZpdHk='; 
            const s2 = 'QHByb3Rvbi5tZQ=='; 
            const targ = atob(s1) + atob(s2);
            window.location.href = 'mailto:' + targ;
        })();">Mailto</button>
```
